purpose: Uses the second derivative of credit (change in new credit flow) to predict growth inflections
components: Non-bank credit offset (private credit, fintech — 25-30% of consumer origination)
Total credit impulse including shadow banking (BIS data)
Track record: correctly predicted 2008, European crisis, 2020 recovery, 2023-2024 resilience
current_assessment: Bank lending credit impulse decelerating since mid-2024. Non-bank credit partially offsets. Total credit impulse still positive but declining. Signals growth deceleration masked by fiscal offsets.
limitations: Measuring total credit impulse including shadow banking is increasingly difficult
Fintech and private credit channels may not respond to same drivers as bank lending
Real-time utility reduced by measurement difficulty
Kalecki fiscal floor may override credit impulse signal
